title: 10 best herbal creams for pertussis

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams helps with pertussis because they contain immunomodulatory compounds that stimulate the body's natural defenses to fight off the Bordetella pertussis bacteria. These creams also possess anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce the severity of coughing fits and ease congestion. Furthermore, Echinacea purpurea has antimicrobial properties that can inhibit the growth and reproduction of the bacteria, thereby reducing the duration and intensity of the pertussis infection. This natural remedy can provide relief and support during the recovery process.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds help boost the immune system by stimulating the production of white blood cells, which are essential in fighting off infections such as pertussis.

Caffeic Acid: As a phenolic compound, caffeic acid has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce the severity of respiratory symptoms associated with pertussis, such as coughing and congestion.

Cichoric Acid: This polyphenol has been shown to have immunomodulatory effects, enhancing the body's natural defense mechanisms against infections, including pertussis, by promoting the production of interferon and other cytokines.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are rich in echinacoside, a compound believed to have anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate symptoms of pertussis.
Flowers: They contain a variety of bioactive compounds, including alkylamides and caffeic acid derivatives, which may exhibit immunomodulatory effects that could help combat pertussis.

Leaves: They are a source of phenolic acids, flavonoids, and other compounds with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that may contribute to the potential therapeutic effects of Echinacea creams for pertussis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh echinacea purpurea flowers in the late summer or early fall when petals start to drop.
Dry the harvested flowers in a dehydrator at 95°f for 8 hours to remove excess moisture completely.
Infuse 1 cup of dried echinacea purpurea flowers in 2 cups of carrier oil like coconut oil at room temperature for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean bowl and discard the solids then refrigerate the oil for 24 hours.
Mix 1/4 cup of the infused oil with 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter in a double boiler for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea angustifolia creams helps with pertussis because of its immunomodulatory properties, which support the body's natural defenses. The plant contains compounds that stimulate the production of white blood cells, enhancing the immune system's ability to fight off infections. In the case of pertussis, also known as whooping cough, Echinacea angustifolia cream may help reduce the severity and duration of the illness by boosting the body's natural resistance to the bacteria that causes it, Bordetella pertussis.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds help by enhancing the body's immune response and reducing inflammation, which can aid in fighting off the Bordetella pertussis bacteria that causes pertussis.

Alkaloids (specifically, Cichoric Acid and Alkylamides): These compounds exhibit antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent the spread of the pertussis bacteria and reduce the severity of symptoms.

Phenolic Acids (specifically, Caffeic Acid and Ferulic Acid): These compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the severity of respiratory symptoms, such as coughing and wheezing, associated with pertussis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used due to their high content of alkylamides and caffeic acid derivatives, which have been shown to have immunomodulatory and anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: Used for their flavonoid content, particularly quercetin, which has been found to exhibit antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties.
Barks: Used for their high content of phenolic compounds, including caffeic acid derivatives and polyphenolic acids, which have been reported to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest echinacea angustifolia roots in late fall after the first frost when the plant is dormant for 2 weeks.
Dry the harvested roots in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours to reduce moisture content to 10%.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder with a medium setting for 30 seconds.
Mix the ground powder with a carrier oil such as coconut oil in a 1:2 ratio by weight for 10 minutes.
Steep the mixture in a double boiler at 100 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours to infuse the oil with the herbal properties.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus creams helps with pertussis because of its decongestant properties, which help relieve the congestion and cough associated with the condition. The eucalyptus oil in the creams acts as an expectorant, thinning mucus and making it easier to cough up, thereby reducing the severity of coughing fits. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of eucalyptus globulus may help soothe the throat and chest, providing relief from the discomfort and irritation caused by pertussis.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): Acts as a bronchodilator, helping to relax airway muscles and reduce cough frequency and severity in pertussis patients.
Caffeic acid: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce inflammation in the airways and alleviate symptoms associated with pertussis.
α-pinene: Possesses expectorant properties, facilitating the removal of mucus from the airways and making it easier for patients with pertussis to cough up the mucus.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain eucalyptol, a primary component of essential oils that has antimicrobial and expectorant properties, which helps relieve cough and congestion.
Barks: They are rich in flavonoids and phenolic compounds, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them useful in treating respiratory issues.
Stems: They contain eucalyptus oil, which has decongestant properties, helping to relieve congestion and cough associated with pertussis.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 1 cup of eucalyptus globulus leaves in 2 cups of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the infused liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl.
In a separate bowl, mix 1/2 cup of beeswax pellets with 1/2 cup of coconut oil.
Combine the infused liquid with the beeswax-coconut oil mixture and heat until fully incorporated.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and allow it to cool and solidify completely.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea pallida creams helps with pertussis because of its immune-boosting properties, which can aid in reducing the duration and severity of the illness. The cream's active compounds, such as alkylamides and phenolic acids, have been shown to stimulate the production of white blood cells, increasing the body's natural defense against bacterial infections. By supporting the immune system, Echinacea pallida creams may help alleviate symptoms of pertussis, such as coughing and fatigue, and promote overall recovery.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the severity and duration of pertussis symptoms.
Alkylphenols: These phenolic compounds have antimicrobial properties, which can help combat the Bordetella pertussis bacteria that causes pertussis.
Triterpenoids: These compounds have immunomodulatory properties, which can help boost the body's immune response and reduce the severity of pertussis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Echinacea pallida are used to create creams for pertussis due to their high content of alkylamides and caffeic acid, which have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: The leaves of Echinacea pallida are used in creams to treat pertussis due to their ability to stimulate the immune system and reduce inflammation.
Flowers: The flowers of Echinacea pallida are used in creams for pertussis due to their high content of phenolic compounds, which exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of dried echinacea pallida roots and flowers from a reputable source.
Grind the dried echinacea pallida into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Combine 1 cup of powdered echinacea pallida with 1/2 cup of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in the double boiler for 10 to 15 minutes or until the beeswax melts.
Allow the mixture to cool and solidify before transferring it into a glass jar.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia creams helps with pertussis because of its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The active compound, tea tree oil, has been shown to inhibit the growth of Bordetella pertussis, the bacteria responsible for whooping cough. Additionally, the cream's soothing and moisturizing properties can help alleviate coughing spasms and ease congestion, making it easier for individuals to breathe and recover. The cream's natural ingredients also promote a healthy skin environment, reducing the risk of skin irritation and complications associated with pertussis.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: It has antimicrobial properties, which help combat bacterial infections that often accompany pertussis.
Linalool: As an antispasmodic, it can help alleviate coughing fits and muscle spasms associated with pertussis.
Terpinen-4-ol: Its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce inflammation and combat secondary infections that can complicate pertussis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Melaleuca alternifolia are the most commonly used part for making creams and ointments due to their high oil content, which is rich in tea tree oil.
Seeds: Melaleuca alternifolia seeds contain a compound called cineole, which is used in some creams and ointments for its antimicrobial properties.
Barks: The bark of Melaleuca alternifolia is sometimes used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be incorporated into creams for pertussis treatment.
remedy_recipe: Melt 1 pound of beeswax in a double boiler at 180-190 degrees fahrenheit for 10 minutes.
Combine 1/2 cup of melaleuca alternifolia essential oil with 1 tablespoon of vitamin e oil in a glass container.
Add 1/4 cup of distilled water to the mixture and stir well for 5 minutes.
Whisk in 1/2 cup of shea butter and 1/4 cup of coconut oil until fully incorporated.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and allow it to cool and thicken for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams helps with pertussis because they contain properties that aid in soothing the respiratory tract and reducing inflammation. The anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties present in the ginger-based creams help in fighting the Bordetella pertussis bacteria, which causes pertussis. Additionally, the warming and expectorant properties of Zingiber officinale help to loosen mucus, making it easier to expel, thereby providing relief from coughing and congestion associated with pertussis. This helps to alleviate symptoms and promote overall recovery.
remedy_constituents: Gingerol: Helps with pertussis by reducing inflammation and relieving respiratory congestion associated with the condition.
Zingiberene: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which aid in relieving cough and congestion caused by pertussis.
Shogaol: Displays antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to combat secondary infections and reduce inflammation associated with pertussis.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds, including gingerol and shogaol, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
Roots: The roots of Zingiber officinale contain similar bioactive compounds to the rhyzomes, making them a viable alternative for producing creams for pertussis.
Buds: The buds of ginger are also used in traditional medicine, and their bioactive compounds may help in alleviating symptoms of pertussis, such as cough and congestion.
remedy_recipe: Combine 2 tablespoons of distilled water with 1 teaspoon of dried zingiber officinale powder in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 5 minutes stirring occasionally to prevent scorching.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl.
Whip 2 ounces of coconut oil with 1 teaspoon of beeswax and 1 teaspoon of shea butter until smooth.
Gradually add the infused water mixture to the whipped coconut oil mixture and whip until combined.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams helps with pertussis because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The licorice root extract in these creams has been shown to soothe irritated mucous membranes and calm coughing spasms, providing relief to patients with pertussis. Additionally, the moisturizing properties of the cream help to keep the skin hydrated, reducing the risk of skin irritation and cracking. The cream's antibacterial properties also help to prevent secondary infections, promoting overall healing and recovery.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins help to reduce inflammation and soothe the respiratory tract, making it easier for patients with pertussis to breathe.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate the symptoms of pertussis, such as coughing and difficulty breathing.
Glycyrrhetic acid: Glycyrrhetic acid has anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which can help loosen mucus and reduce the severity of coughing fits in patients with pertussis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used due to their rich content of glycyrrhizin, which is known for its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, helping to relieve cough symptoms.
Leaves: Utilized for their secondary metabolites, such as glycosides, which may aid in reducing inflammation and facilitating mucus production.
Barks: Employed for their flavonoids and saponins, which have been reported to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, helping to soothe coughs and respiratory issues.
remedy_recipe: Extract 30 grams of dried root of glycyrrhiza glabra in 500 ml of cold water for 30 minutes to 1 hour.
Filter the mixture through cheesecloth into a bowl to separate the liquid from the solids.
Combine 30 grams of glycerin and 10 grams of distilled water with the filtered liquid in a beaker.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler to 60-70 degrees celsius for 20-30 minutes or until it thickens.
Allow the mixture to cool and then whip it with a hand mixer until it becomes a smooth cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris creams helps with pertussis because they contain thymol, an antimicrobial compound that inhibits the growth of Bordetella pertussis, the bacteria responsible for the disease. The thymol also has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce congestion and coughing, making it easier for the body to fight off the infection. Additionally, thymus extracts have been shown to stimulate the immune system, allowing the body to recover more quickly from the illness. This natural approach can be a valuable complement to conventional treatment.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Thymol has antimicrobial properties that can help combat the bacterial infection Bordetella pertussis, which causes whooping cough.
Caryophyllene oxide: Caryophyllene oxide has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the lungs, which is a common complication of pertussis.

Bornyl acetate: Bornyl acetate has expectorant properties that can help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to breathe and reducing the severity of coughing fits associated with pertussis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Thyme leaves are often used due to their antiseptic and expectorant properties, which help alleviate coughs and congestion associated with pertussis.
Buds: Thyme buds are used for their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can aid in reducing the severity of coughs and respiratory issues.
Flowers: Thyme flowers are used due to their expectorant and antispasmodic properties, which can help relieve coughs and congestion, and also reduce muscle spasms in the respiratory system.
remedy_recipe: Harvest thymus vulgaris leaves in the morning when they are at their highest oil content and freshness.
Dry the harvested leaves in a single layer at room temperature for 7 to 10 days.
Combine 2 cups of dried thymus vulgaris leaves with 1 cup of distilled water in a saucepan.
Infuse the mixture for 30 to 40 minutes over low heat then strain through cheesecloth.
Mix 1/2 cup of beeswax with 1/4 cup of coconut oil and 1/4 cup of the thymus vulgaris infusion.

remedies.remedy_desc: Sambucus nigra creams helps with pertussis because they contain high concentrations of antiviral compounds and antioxidants, which can help to boost the immune system and combat the Bordetella pertussis bacteria. The Sambucus nigra extract has been traditionally used to treat respiratory infections and colds, and its anti-inflammatory properties can help to reduce the severity of symptoms associated with pertussis, such as coughing and fever. Additionally, Sambucus nigra creams may help to reduce the duration of the illness.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These constituents exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce the severity and duration of pertussis symptoms.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids in Sambucus nigra have been shown to possess immunomodulatory and antiviral activities, which can help alleviate the symptoms of pertussis by modulating the immune response and inhibiting the replication of the pertussis virus.

Tannins: Tannins in Sambucus nigra possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the severity of pertussis symptoms and support the body's natural defense mechanisms against the pertussis virus.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their antitussive and expectorant properties, which help relieve coughing.
Leaves: They are used due to their antiviral and antimicrobial properties, which help combat the underlying infection causing pertussis.
Buds: They are used due to their expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help relieve respiratory symptoms associated with pertussis.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 250ml of water with 20g of dried sambucus nigra flowers in a heat-proof bowl for 10 minutes.
Strain the infused liquid through a cheesecloth into a bowl discarding the solids immediately after.
Combine 50g of candelilla wax 20g of beeswax and 20g of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Add the infused sambucus nigra liquid to the melted wax mixture stirring constantly for 5 minutes.
Pour the mixture into a tin and allow it to set at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Astragalus membranaceus creams helps with pertussis because of its immunomodulatory and anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's active compounds, including astragaloside and polysaccharides, stimulate the immune system, enhancing its ability to fight off the Bordetella pertussis bacteria that cause whooping cough. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory effects help soothe and calm the respiratory tract, reducing inflammation and congestion associated with the disease. This natural approach may help alleviate symptoms and support the body's recovery process.
remedy_constituents: Trigonelline: Helps by stimulating the body's immune response to fight off the pertussis bacteria, reducing the severity and duration of the disease.
Saponins: Contributes by reducing inflammation in the respiratory tract and relieving coughing spasms, providing relief from pertussis symptoms.
Flavonoids: Plays a role by enhancing the body's antioxidant defenses, protecting the respiratory system from oxidative damage caused by pertussis, and promoting overall recovery.
remedy_parts: Roots: Astragalus root is often used in traditional medicine due to its adaptogenic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: Astragalus root may also be used for its immunomodulatory effects.
Roots: Astragalus root has been traditionally used to help support lung health, which might be relevant for conditions like pertussis, though this is not a direct use for creams.
remedy_recipe: Extract 30g of dried astragalus membranaceus root in a 1:5 ratio with 150ml of 95% ethanol for 2 hours.
Filter the extraction through a buchner funnel to obtain 30ml of liquid extract in 10 minutes.
Mix 10ml of liquid extract with 10ml of distilled water and 5g of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 60-70°c for 30 minutes to create a smooth and consistent texture in 30 minutes.
Pour the mixture into a tin container and allow it to cool and solidify at room temperature in 1 hour.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for pertussis are topical preparations that utilize plant extracts to alleviate symptoms and support the body's natural recovery process from whooping cough. These creams are particularly beneficial in soothing coughs, reducing inflammation, and promoting healing. The most effective herbal creams for pertussis include Echinacea purpurea, Echinacea angustifolia, Eucalyptus globulus, Echinacea pallida, Melaleuca alternifolia, Zingiber officinale, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Thymus vulgaris, Sambucus nigra, and Astragalus membranaceus. If left untreated, pertussis can lead to severe respiratory complications, hospitalizations, and long-lasting effects on an individual's quality of life, impacting daily routines, relationships, and overall well-being.
